notes: Practical air-filtration equipment on a controlled job site — HEPA air scrubbers, negative-pressure containment, ducting, filter management, monitoring. Australian settings, metric where shown, 240 V/RCD leads. Bright, clean, professional. No on-image text, no real brand names/logos.

images.context: Course hero image for 'Using Air Scrubbers and AFDs to Improve Job Site Air Quality'
images.prompt: A pair of HEPA air scrubbers set up on a controlled Australian restoration job site inside a clear plastic containment zone, one unit ducted to a window to establish negative pressure. A technician in a P100 respirator, gloves and disposable coveralls adjusts the speed dial on a unit, power leads run tidily along the skirting. Bright, clean, professional, teal and blue tones, orderly workflow. Over-shoulder framing, realistic photography.
images.negativePrompt: no on-image text, no logos, no brand names, no watermarks, no gore, no distressing/dramatic mould, no courtroom, no identifiable face-forward portraits, no tripping hazards, no imperial units

images.context: Modules on filter types and maintenance — multi-stage pre-filter and HEPA
images.prompt: Close-up of a gloved technician sliding a fresh pleated HEPA filter into an open air scrubber housing, a soiled pre-filter set aside in a sealed clear bag ready for disposal. Clean workshop bench, bright even lighting, the multi-stage filtration clearly visible, methodical maintenance scene. Hands-on-task framing, realistic photography.

images.context: Modules on negative pressure setup and monitoring effectiveness — ducting and instruments
images.prompt: A flexible exhaust duct from an air scrubber sealed neatly through a window opening of an Australian building to vent filtered air outside, while a gloved hand holds a digital manometer confirming the pressure differential at the containment barrier. A particle counter rests on a nearby crate. Bright, controlled, professional containment scene. Mid-distance framing, realistic photography.
